calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joeseph Ferrucci appeared via zoom for and with Ryan Brent Yee (petitioner).

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated September 23, 2013 is admitted to probate and each codicil dated March 25, 2014 is admitted to probate and Ryan Brent Yee is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for October 19, 2027 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status on administration is filed two weeks prior to the hearing date, the status hearing will go off calendar. Michael Kasolas is appointed Probate Referee. Disclosure: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Fajardo. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-17-2026 Continued To Apr-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Continued To Mar-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Continued To Feb-10-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Ferrucci for petitioner Ryan Brent Yee, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary.

Mr. Ferrucci confirmed that a codicil had been discovered but was believed to be in the possession of the decedent's half-brother Ivan Yee. Mr. Ferrucci explained that Ivan was encouraged to obtain legal counsel and lodge the document with the Court. So far, the codicil had not yet been produced.

The Court suggested that counsel file a petition under Probate Code section 8200 to compel production of the codicil.

Following discussion, the matter was continued to February 10, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time to file and serve a petition under Probate Code Section 8200.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Continued To Oct-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joseph Ferrucci for petitioner and proposed executor Ryan Brent Yee, who was also present.

The Court called the matter on the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary.

The Court and the parties discussed whether the "VOID" marking on the will constituted a canceled will under Probate Code section 6120(b).

Mr. Ferrucci explained that the will was found in a sealed box of the decedent's estate planning documents, which also contained prior superseded instruments. Mr. Ferrucci argued case law requires more definitive acts such as cross-outs for cancellation, and that a single "VOID" notation was insufficient. Mr. Ferrucci referenced case law dating back to Olmstead (1898) and offered to provide supplemental briefing.

The Court directed Mr. Ferrucci to file a supplemental brief by October 14, 2025, and continued the matter to October 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
